name: Bangor Savings Bank
description_short: Regional bank operating 70+ branches across Maine and New Hampshire since 1852, offering checking, savings, credit cards, home loans, and business banking services.
description_long:
Bangor Savings Bank is a community-focused financial institution founded in 1852 that has built its reputation on local investment and customer relationships across Northern New England. The bank operates 70 retail branch locations throughout Maine and New Hampshire, supported by an extensive ATM network, making it a significant regional banking presence with deep historical roots in the communities it serves.

The bank offers comprehensive retail and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), home loans, personal loans, credit cards (everblue branded), business banking solutions, payroll services, and wealth management. They also provide teen banking products through pling, a debit card and app designed for teens, along with financial education resources through their "Strengthen Your Money Skills" program covering seven financial modules. Digital banking options include Bangor Online portal, a mobile app, and specialized business platforms like Treasury & Business Online.

Bangor Savings Bank distinguishes itself through active community investment via The Bangor Savings Bank Foundation, which has awarded significant grants to nonprofits (over $86K in 2025 alone). The bank emphasizes community partnerships, volunteerism, and corporate funding as core values. Their everblue credit card line offers multiple options tailored to different needs: 2% cash back on local purchases, credit-building features, interest savings benefits, and business expense management tools. The bank also highlights security infrastructure and hosts an annual April voting initiative where customers help select nonprofit grant recipients.

As a regional bank, Bangor Savings Bank is best suited for customers in Maine and New Hampshire seeking personalized service with local branch access rather than a national institution. The primary limitation is geographic—service is concentrated in Northern New England with no presence in other U.S. regions. While the bank offers competitive services across retail and business segments, customers outside their service area cannot access accounts. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: 70 retail branch locations plus extensive ATM network throughout Maine and New Hampshire for convenient local access
Multi-option everblue credit card line including 2% cash back on local purchases, credit-building, and business cash back variants
Active community investment through The Bangor Savings Bank Foundation with documented grant-making (over $86K awarded in 2025)
Teen banking product (pling) with parent controls and financial education features specifically designed for younger users
Comprehensive digital banking including Bangor Online, mobile app, and specialized Treasury & Business Online platform
Free financial education program (Strengthen Your Money Skills) with seven self-paced modules
Long institutional history (operating since 1852) providing stability and deep local community relationships
cons: Limited geographic service area—only operates in Maine and New Hampshire, excluding customers in other states
Website does not prominently display specific rates for checking, savings, or loan products, requiring customers to contact bank directly
No information provided about minimum deposit requirements, monthly fees, or account maintenance costs
Limited transparency on home loan and business loan terms, products, and qualification requirements from website alone
best_for: Maine and New Hampshire residents seeking personalized banking with local branch relationships and community investment
Business owners in the region needing comprehensive business banking, payroll, and cash management solutions
Families with teens interested in financial education and supervised debit card products
Customers prioritizing community reinvestment and foundation grant-making as part of banking values
diagnosis: Bangor Savings Bank is ideal for Maine and New Hampshire residents prioritizing local community banking with personalized service, branch accessibility, and reinvestment in their communities. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—customers outside Maine and New Hampshire cannot access accounts, making this bank unsuitable for multi-state or national customers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
